filters.notes: Nearly full scan; date threshold computed via date_utils.h — C1/C7: NEVER hardcode or use N×365

data_structures.notes: 4 valid (rf,ls) group combos; rf has 3 codes [0,1,2], ls has 2 codes [0,1] → index = rf_code*2+ls_code ∈ [0,5], 4 slots active. 4 slots × ~80 bytes = 320 bytes per thread; 64 threads × 320B = 20KB total → fits in L1 cache. Zero hashing overhead.

scan_strategy.rationale: 98.5% selectivity — selection vector overhead not worthwhile; fuse filter + aggregate in one tight loop over lineitem rows
scan_strategy.zone_map_usage: load l_shipdate_zone_map; skip blocks where blocks[b].mn > threshold (block guaranteed no-pass); for boundary blocks (mn<=threshold<mx) scan normally; with 98.5% selectivity almost no blocks skipped — still apply for correctness and boundary handling

precision_rules.sum_qty: double — max group sum ~50M×50=2.5×10^9, well under 10^13
precision_rules.sum_base_price: long double — max group sum ~50M×104949≈5×10^12, use long double for safety
precision_rules.sum_disc_price: long double — C35: multi-column derived expression, MUST NOT use int64_t cents
precision_rules.sum_charge: long double — C35: multi-column derived expression, MUST NOT use int64_t cents
precision_rules.sum_disc: double — values in [0.00, 0.10], sum magnitude negligible
precision_rules.avg_computation: computed post-merge as sum/count, not incrementally

aggregation.merge_rationale: T×G = 64×4 = 256 — trivially small; sequential loop over 64 thread-local arrays, 4 slots each = 256 additions total

parallelism.pattern: each thread maintains own 4-slot AggSlot[6] local array; parallel scan over lineitem row morsels; single sequential merge after barrier
parallelism.thread_local_init: zero-initialize AggSlot[6] per thread before scan loop

indexes.skip_condition: blocks[b].mn > threshold → skip entire block (0 rows pass)
indexes.partial_condition: blocks[b].mn <= threshold AND blocks[b].mx > threshold → scan normally
indexes.full_pass_condition: blocks[b].mx <= threshold → all rows pass, no per-row date check needed
indexes.expected_benefit: minimal at 98.5% selectivity — nearly all blocks must be scanned; still apply for boundary blocks and full-pass optimization

date_handling.init: gendb::init_date_tables() — call ONCE at top of main() before any date operations (C11)

dictionary_handling.l_returnflag: load lineitem/l_returnflag_dict.txt into std::vector<std::string> rf_dict at runtime — C2: NEVER hardcode codes
dictionary_handling.l_linestatus: load lineitem/l_linestatus_dict.txt into std::vector<std::string> ls_dict at runtime — C2: NEVER hardcode codes
dictionary_handling.csv_quoting: C31: double-quote ALL string output columns — printf("\"%s\"", str.c_str())

output.sort_on: string labels decoded from rf_dict/ls_dict after merge
output.format: CSV with %.2f for monetary/quantity columns, %lld for count
